It is located in [[Hida Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|WD=Q845945}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|WD=Q134917286}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|WD=Q135022904}} * {{ill|Q135160342|lt=Q135160342|WD=Q135160342}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11665887|lt=Q11665887|WD=Q11665887}} ** Series ordinal: 3 ** P155: {{ill|Q11542024|lt=Q11542024|WD=Q11542024}} ** P156: {{ill|Ohotsuno Shrine|lt=Ohotsuno Shrine|WD=Q135039910}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q622594|lt=Q622594|WD=Q622594}} * {{ill|Q1056535|lt=Q1056535|WD=Q1056535}} * {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|lt=Ono district|ceb|Ōno Gun|en|Ōno District, Gifu|es|Distrito de Ōno (Gifu)|fa|شهرستان اونو، گیفو|fr|District d'Ōno|id|Distrik Ōno, Gifu|it|Distretto di Ōno|ja|大野郡|ko|오노군|pl|Powiat Ōno|pt|Distrito de Ono (Gifu)|th|อำเภอโอโนะ (จังหวัดกิฟุ)|uk|Повіт Оно (Ґіфу)|zh|大野郡|WD=Q31671}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.12075|137.290361}} * {{coord|36.120725|137.290398}} == image (P18) == * Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (14).jpg == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県高山市江名子町4946', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kōjinja (Takayama)}} [[JA:荒神社 (高山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11618837}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=荒神社 | 画像=[[File:Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (14).jpg|250px]]
拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|高山市|Q622594}}江名子町4946 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=火結神 {{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火之夜藝速男神}}　など | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（論社） | 例祭={{jalink|閏年|Q19828}}1月7日前後 | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=荒神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (24).jpg|thumb|250px|荒神社]] '''荒神社'''（こうじんじゃ）は{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|高山市|Q622594}}江名子町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。「あらがみしゃ」「あらじんじゃ」とも呼称される。 {{jalink|飛騨国|Q1056535}}{{jalink|大野郡|Q31671}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}、荏名神社の{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}である。式内・荏名神社は同じ高山市江名子町の{{jalink|荏名神社|Q11618316}}とされているが、これは{{jalink|文化 (元号)|Q1373146|文化}}12年（{{jalink|1815年|Q6948}}）、高山の国学者{{jalink|田中大秀|Q6536652}}が、江名子町の稲置（いなき）の森にあった子安大明神を、「荏名」が「{{jalink|胎盤|Q1212935|胞衣}}」と解釈されたためだとして式内・荏名神社に比定したものである。現在も荒神社が荏名とする説が強い。 {{jalink|神通川|Q1156518|宮川}}の支流、江名子川の上流に位置する。江名子という地名が荏名から転じたという説がある。 創建時期は不明だが、かつては女人禁制の聖地であったという。 === 祭神 === *火結神 *{{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火之夜藝速男神}} *奧津日子神 *奧津日賣神 === 例祭 === *4年に1度（{{jalink|閏年|Q19828}}の1月7日前後の土・日曜日）に行われる祭り。あまざけ祭りともいう。 *元々は{{jalink|陰暦|Q28692535}}閏年の11月18日であったが、明治以降{{jalink|新暦|Q125314718}}になってからは閏年1月7日に変更された。現在の日程になったのは戦後である。 *{{jalink|甘酒|Q455990}}と五穀餅（米、麦、大豆、栗、小豆）が参拝者にふるまわれる。 *かつては、田んぼに{{jalink|注連縄|Q1887421}}を張り、焚火で飯を炊いてその場所で甘酒を仕込み、さめないようにして一夜その田んぼに置いて、翌朝甘酒の出来具合でその年の豊凶を占っていた。現在は上江名子公民館の前で甘酒を仕込む。 === 文化財 === *荒神社の夫婦スギ（市指定天然記念物）{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/data/kojinjyameotosugi.htm |title=荒神社の夫婦スギ |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2013-04-21}} === 交通 === *{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|高山駅|Q874020}}より南東へ約5km。 **飛騨ふる里トンネルの北、約1km。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|飛騨国の式内社一覧|Q11665887}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや たかやま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]]

panoramio (24).jpg|thumb|250px|荒神社]] '''荒神社'''（こうじんじゃ）は{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|高山市|Q622594}}江名子町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。「あらがみしゃ」「あらじんじゃ」とも呼称される。 {{jalink|飛騨国|Q1056535}}{{jalink|大野郡|Q31671}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}、荏名神社の{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}である。式内・荏名神社は同じ高山市江名子町の{{jalink|荏名神社|Q11618316}}とされているが、これは{{jalink|文化 (元号)|Q1373146|文化}}12年（{{jalink|1815年|Q6948}}）、高山の国学者{{jalink|田中大秀|Q6536652}}が、江名子町の稲置（いなき）の森にあった子安大明神を、「荏名」が「{{jalink|胎盤|Q1212935|胞衣}}」と解釈されたためだとして式内・荏名神社に比定したものである。現在も荒神社が荏名とする説が強い。 {{jalink|神通川|Q1156518|宮川}}の支流、江名子川の上流に位置する。江名子という地名が荏名から転じたという説がある。 創建時期は不明だが、かつては女人禁制の聖地であったという。 === 祭神 === *火結神 *{{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火之夜藝速男神}} *奧津日子神 *奧津日賣神 === 例祭 === *4年に1度（{{jalink|閏年|Q19828}}の1月7日前後の土・日曜日）に行われる祭り。あまざけ祭りともいう。 *元々は{{jalink|陰暦|Q28692535}}閏年の11月18日であったが、明治以降{{jalink|新暦|Q125314718}}になってからは閏年1月7日に変更された。現在の日程になったのは戦後である。 *{{jalink|甘酒|Q455990}}と五穀餅（米、麦、大豆、栗、小豆）が参拝者にふるまわれる。 *かつては、田んぼに{{jalink|注連縄|Q1887421}}を張り、焚火で飯を炊いてその場所で甘酒を仕込み、さめないようにして一夜その田んぼに置いて、翌朝甘酒の出来具合でその年の豊凶を占っていた。現在は上江名子公民館の前で甘酒を仕込む。 === 文化財 === *荒神社の夫婦スギ（市指定天然記念物）{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/data/kojinjyameotosugi.htm |title=荒神社の夫婦スギ |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2013-04-21}} === 交通 === *{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|高山駅|Q874020}}より南東へ約5km。 **飛騨ふる里トンネルの北、約1km。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|飛騨国の式内社一覧|Q11665887}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや たかやま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:need translation]]

panoramio (24).jpg|thumb|250px|荒神社]] '''荒神社'''（こうじんじゃ）は{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|高山市|Q622594}}江名子町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。「あらがみしゃ」「あらじんじゃ」とも呼称される。 {{jalink|飛騨国|Q1056535}}{{jalink|大野郡|Q31671}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}、荏名神社の{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}である。式内・荏名神社は同じ高山市江名子町の{{jalink|荏名神社|Q11618316}}とされているが、これは{{jalink|文化 (元号)|Q1373146|文化}}12年（{{jalink|1815年|Q6948}}）、高山の国学者{{jalink|田中大秀|Q6536652}}が、江名子町の稲置（いなき）の森にあった子安大明神を、「荏名」が「{{jalink|胎盤|Q1212935|胞衣}}」と解釈されたためだとして式内・荏名神社に比定したものである。現在も荒神社が荏名とする説が強い。 {{jalink|神通川|Q1156518|宮川}}の支流、江名子川の上流に位置する。江名子という地名が荏名から転じたという説がある。 創建時期は不明だが、かつては女人禁制の聖地であったという。 === 祭神 === *火結神 *{{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火之夜藝速男神}} *奧津日子神 *奧津日賣神 === 例祭 === *4年に1度（{{jalink|閏年|Q19828}}の1月7日前後の土・日曜日）に行われる祭り。あまざけ祭りともいう。 *元々は{{jalink|陰暦|Q28692535}}閏年の11月18日であったが、明治以降{{jalink|新暦|Q125314718}}になってからは閏年1月7日に変更された。現在の日程になったのは戦後である。 *{{jalink|甘酒|Q455990}}と五穀餅（米、麦、大豆、栗、小豆）が参拝者にふるまわれる。 *かつては、田んぼに{{jalink|注連縄|Q1887421}}を張り、焚火で飯を炊いてその場所で甘酒を仕込み、さめないようにして一夜その田んぼに置いて、翌朝甘酒の出来具合でその年の豊凶を占っていた。現在は上江名子公民館の前で甘酒を仕込む。 === 文化財 === *荒神社の夫婦スギ（市指定天然記念物）{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/data/kojinjyameotosugi.htm |title=荒神社の夫婦スギ |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2013-04-21}} === 交通 === *{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|高山駅|Q874020}}より南東へ約5km。 **飛騨ふる里トンネルの北、約1km。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|飛騨国の式内社一覧|Q11665887}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや たかやま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:need translation]]

panoramio (24).jpg|thumb|250px|荒神社]] '''荒神社'''（こうじんじゃ）は{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|高山市|Q622594}}江名子町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。「あらがみしゃ」「あらじんじゃ」とも呼称される。 {{jalink|飛騨国|Q1056535}}{{jalink|大野郡|Q31671}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}、荏名神社の{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}である。式内・荏名神社は同じ高山市江名子町の{{jalink|荏名神社|Q11618316}}とされているが、これは{{jalink|文化 (元号)|Q1373146|文化}}12年（{{jalink|1815年|Q6948}}）、高山の国学者{{jalink|田中大秀|Q6536652}}が、江名子町の稲置（いなき）の森にあった子安大明神を、「荏名」が「{{jalink|胎盤|Q1212935|胞衣}}」と解釈されたためだとして式内・荏名神社に比定したものである。現在も荒神社が荏名とする説が強い。 {{jalink|神通川|Q1156518|宮川}}の支流、江名子川の上流に位置する。江名子という地名が荏名から転じたという説がある。 創建時期は不明だが、かつては女人禁制の聖地であったという。 === 祭神 === *火結神 *{{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火之夜藝速男神}} *奧津日子神 *奧津日賣神 === 例祭 === *4年に1度（{{jalink|閏年|Q19828}}の1月7日前後の土・日曜日）に行われる祭り。あまざけ祭りともいう。 *元々は{{jalink|陰暦|Q28692535}}閏年の11月18日であったが、明治以降{{jalink|新暦|Q125314718}}になってからは閏年1月7日に変更された。現在の日程になったのは戦後である。 *{{jalink|甘酒|Q455990}}と五穀餅（米、麦、大豆、栗、小豆）が参拝者にふるまわれる。 *かつては、田んぼに{{jalink|注連縄|Q1887421}}を張り、焚火で飯を炊いてその場所で甘酒を仕込み、さめないようにして一夜その田んぼに置いて、翌朝甘酒の出来具合でその年の豊凶を占っていた。現在は上江名子公民館の前で甘酒を仕込む。 === 文化財 === *荒神社の夫婦スギ（市指定天然記念物）{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/data/kojinjyameotosugi.htm |title=荒神社の夫婦スギ |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2013-04-21}} === 交通 === *{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|高山駅|Q874020}}より南東へ約5km。 **飛騨ふる里トンネルの北、約1km。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|飛騨国の式内社一覧|Q11665887}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや たかやま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kō Shrine (Q11618837)}}

panoramio (24).jpg|thumb|250px|荒神社]] '''荒神社'''（こうじんじゃ）は{{jalink|岐阜県|Q131277}}{{jalink|高山市|Q622594}}江名子町にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。「あらがみしゃ」「あらじんじゃ」とも呼称される。 {{jalink|飛騨国|Q1056535}}{{jalink|大野郡|Q31671}}の{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}、荏名神社の{{jalink|論社|Q135022904}}である。式内・荏名神社は同じ高山市江名子町の{{jalink|荏名神社|Q11618316}}とされているが、これは{{jalink|文化 (元号)|Q1373146|文化}}12年（{{jalink|1815年|Q6948}}）、高山の国学者{{jalink|田中大秀|Q6536652}}が、江名子町の稲置（いなき）の森にあった子安大明神を、「荏名」が「{{jalink|胎盤|Q1212935|胞衣}}」と解釈されたためだとして式内・荏名神社に比定したものである。現在も荒神社が荏名とする説が強い。 {{jalink|神通川|Q1156518|宮川}}の支流、江名子川の上流に位置する。江名子という地名が荏名から転じたという説がある。 創建時期は不明だが、かつては女人禁制の聖地であったという。 === 祭神 === *火結神 *{{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火之夜藝速男神}} *奧津日子神 *奧津日賣神 === 例祭 === *4年に1度（{{jalink|閏年|Q19828}}の1月7日前後の土・日曜日）に行われる祭り。あまざけ祭りともいう。 *元々は{{jalink|陰暦|Q28692535}}閏年の11月18日であったが、明治以降{{jalink|新暦|Q125314718}}になってからは閏年1月7日に変更された。現在の日程になったのは戦後である。 *{{jalink|甘酒|Q455990}}と五穀餅（米、麦、大豆、栗、小豆）が参拝者にふるまわれる。 *かつては、田んぼに{{jalink|注連縄|Q1887421}}を張り、焚火で飯を炊いてその場所で甘酒を仕込み、さめないようにして一夜その田んぼに置いて、翌朝甘酒の出来具合でその年の豊凶を占っていた。現在は上江名子公民館の前で甘酒を仕込む。 === 文化財 === *荒神社の夫婦スギ（市指定天然記念物）{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/data/kojinjyameotosugi.htm |title=荒神社の夫婦スギ |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2013-04-21}} === 交通 === *{{jalink|高山本線|Q1188357}}{{jalink|高山駅|Q874020}}より南東へ約5km。 **飛騨ふる里トンネルの北、約1km。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|飛騨国の式内社一覧|Q11665887}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや たかやま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kō Shrine (Q11618837)}}

{{Expand Japanese|荒神社 (高山市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kō Shrine (Q11618837) | native_name = 荒神社 | image = Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (24).jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Kō Shrine (Q11618837) | coordinates = {{coord|36.12075|137.290361}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Kō Shrine'''|荒神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Hida Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|WD=Q845945}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|WD=Q134917286}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|WD=Q135022904}} * {{ill|Q135160342|lt=Q135160342|WD=Q135160342}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11665887|lt=Q11665887|WD=Q11665887}} ** Series ordinal: 3 ** P155: {{ill|Q11542024|lt=Q11542024|WD=Q11542024}} ** P156: {{ill|Ohotsuno Shrine|lt=Ohotsuno Shrine|WD=Q135039910}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q622594|lt=Q622594|WD=Q622594}} * {{ill|Q1056535|lt=Q1056535|WD=Q1056535}} * {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|lt=Ono district|ceb|Ōno Gun|en|Ōno District, Gifu|es|Distrito de Ōno (Gifu)|fa|شهرستان اونو، گیفو|fr|District d'Ōno|id|Distrik Ōno, Gifu|it|Distretto di Ōno|ja|大野郡|ko|오노군|pl|Powiat Ōno|pt|Distrito de Ono (Gifu)|th|อำเภอโอโนะ (จังหวัดกิฟุ)|uk|Повіт Оно (Ґіфу)|zh|大野郡|WD=Q31671}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.12075|137.290361}} * {{coord|36.120725|137.290398}} == image (P18) == * Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (24).jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181963 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|WD=Q135159299}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11071125|lt=Q11071125|WD=Q11071125}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9200005009982 == Commons category (P373) == * Kōjinja (Takayama) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Enano Shrine|lt=Enano Shrine|WD=Q135039909}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == image of interior (P5775) == * Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (14).jpg == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県高山市江名子町4946', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kōjinja (Takayama)}} [[JA:荒神社 (高山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11618837}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kō Shrine | 画像=[[File:Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (14).jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Takayama|Q622594}} Enako-chō 4946 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Hi-musubi-no-kami {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami}} etc. | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (candidate shrine) | 例祭=around January 7 of {{jalink|leap years|Q19828}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kō Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (24).jpg|thumb|250px|Kō Shrine]] '''Kō Shrine''' (こうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Enako-chō, {{jalink|Takayama|Q622594}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is also called “Aragami-sha” or “Ara-jinja.” It is a candidate shrine for Ena Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Ōno District|Q31671}}, {{jalink|Hida Province|Q1056535}}. The Shikinaisha Ena Shrine is usually taken as {{jalink|Ena Shrine|Q11618316}} in the same Enako-chō, Takayama, but this identification was made in {{jalink|Bunka era|Q1373146|Bunka}} 12 ({{jalink|1815|Q6948}}) by the Takayama kokugaku scholar {{jalink|Tanaka Ōhidesu|Q6536652}}, who identified Koyasu Daimyōjin in Inaki no Mori, Enako-chō, as the Shikinaisha Ena Shrine because “Ena” was interpreted as {{jalink|placenta|Q1212935|胞衣}}. Even now, the theory that Kō Shrine is Ena remains strong. It is located upstream on the Enako River, a tributary of the {{jalink|Miya River|Q1156518|Miya River}} in the Jinzu system. There is a theory that the place name Enako changed from Ena. The founding period is unknown, but it is said to have once been a sacred place where women were prohibited. === Enshrined deities === *Hi-musubi-no-kami *{{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami}} *Okutsu-hiko-no-Kami *Okutsu-hime-no-Kami === Annual festival === *A festival held once every four years (around Saturday/Sunday near January 7 in leap years). Also called the amazake festival. *Originally on lunar leap-year November 18, but after Meiji and the solar calendar it changed to leap-year January 7. The current schedule dates from after WWII. *{{jalink|Amazake|Q455990}} and gogoku mochi (rice, barley, soybeans, chestnuts, azuki beans) are served to worshippers. *Formerly, a {{jalink|shimenawa|Q1887421}} was hung in a rice field, rice was cooked over a fire, amazake was prepared there and left overnight in the field to keep warm, and the quality next morning was used to divine that year’s harvest. Today amazake is prepared in front of Kami-Enako Community Hall. === Cultural properties === *Meoto cedar trees of Kō Shrine (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/data/kojinjyameotosugi.htm |title=荒神社の夫婦スギ |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-21}} === Access === *About 5 km southeast from {{jalink|Takayama Station|Q874020}} on the {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}}. **About 1 km north of Hida Furusato Tunnel. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|Q11665887}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや たかやま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kō Shrine (Q11618837)}}

{{Expand Japanese|荒神社 (高山市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kō Shrine (Q11618837) | native_name = 荒神社 | image = Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (24).jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Kō Shrine (Q11618837) | coordinates = {{coord|36.12075|137.290361}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Kō Shrine'''|荒神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Hida Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|WD=Q845945}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|WD=Q134917286}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|WD=Q135022904}} * {{ill|Q135160342|lt=Q135160342|WD=Q135160342}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11665887|lt=Q11665887|WD=Q11665887}} ** Series ordinal: 3 ** P155: {{ill|Q11542024|lt=Q11542024|WD=Q11542024}} ** P156: {{ill|Ohotsuno Shrine|lt=Ohotsuno Shrine|WD=Q135039910}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q622594|lt=Q622594|WD=Q622594}} * {{ill|Q1056535|lt=Q1056535|WD=Q1056535}} * {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|lt=Ono district|ceb|Ōno Gun|en|Ōno District, Gifu|es|Distrito de Ōno (Gifu)|fa|شهرستان اونو، گیفو|fr|District d'Ōno|id|Distrik Ōno, Gifu|it|Distretto di Ōno|ja|大野郡|ko|오노군|pl|Powiat Ōno|pt|Distrito de Ono (Gifu)|th|อำเภอโอโนะ (จังหวัดกิฟุ)|uk|Повіт Оно (Ґіфу)|zh|大野郡|WD=Q31671}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.12075|137.290361}} * {{coord|36.120725|137.290398}} == image (P18) == * Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (24).jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181963 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|WD=Q135159299}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11071125|lt=Q11071125|WD=Q11071125}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9200005009982 == Commons category (P373) == * Kōjinja (Takayama) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Enano Shrine|lt=Enano Shrine|WD=Q135039909}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == image of interior (P5775) == * Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (14).jpg == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県高山市江名子町4946', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kōjinja (Takayama)}} [[JA:荒神社 (高山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11618837}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kō Shrine | 画像=[[File:Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (14).jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Takayama|Q622594}} Enako-chō 4946 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Hi-musubi-no-kami {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami}} etc. | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (candidate shrine) | 例祭=around January 7 of {{jalink|leap years|Q19828}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kō Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (24).jpg|thumb|250px|Kō Shrine]] '''Kō Shrine''' (こうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Enako-chō, {{jalink|Takayama|Q622594}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is also called “Aragami-sha” or “Ara-jinja.” It is a candidate shrine for Ena Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Ōno District|Q31671}}, {{jalink|Hida Province|Q1056535}}. The Shikinaisha Ena Shrine is usually taken as {{jalink|Ena Shrine|Q11618316}} in the same Enako-chō, Takayama, but this identification was made in {{jalink|Bunka era|Q1373146|Bunka}} 12 ({{jalink|1815|Q6948}}) by the Takayama kokugaku scholar {{jalink|Tanaka Ōhidesu|Q6536652}}, who identified Koyasu Daimyōjin in Inaki no Mori, Enako-chō, as the Shikinaisha Ena Shrine because “Ena” was interpreted as {{jalink|placenta|Q1212935|胞衣}}. Even now, the theory that Kō Shrine is Ena remains strong. It is located upstream on the Enako River, a tributary of the {{jalink|Miya River|Q1156518|Miya River}} in the Jinzu system. There is a theory that the place name Enako changed from Ena. The founding period is unknown, but it is said to have once been a sacred place where women were prohibited. === Enshrined deities === *Hi-musubi-no-kami *{{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami}} *Okutsu-hiko-no-Kami *Okutsu-hime-no-Kami === Annual festival === *A festival held once every four years (around Saturday/Sunday near January 7 in leap years). Also called the amazake festival. *Originally on lunar leap-year November 18, but after Meiji and the solar calendar it changed to leap-year January 7. The current schedule dates from after WWII. *{{jalink|Amazake|Q455990}} and gogoku mochi (rice, barley, soybeans, chestnuts, azuki beans) are served to worshippers. *Formerly, a {{jalink|shimenawa|Q1887421}} was hung in a rice field, rice was cooked over a fire, amazake was prepared there and left overnight in the field to keep warm, and the quality next morning was used to divine that year’s harvest. Today amazake is prepared in front of Kami-Enako Community Hall. === Cultural properties === *Meoto cedar trees of Kō Shrine (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/data/kojinjyameotosugi.htm |title=荒神社の夫婦スギ |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-21}} === Access === *About 5 km southeast from {{jalink|Takayama Station|Q874020}} on the {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}}. **About 1 km north of Hida Furusato Tunnel. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|Q11665887}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや たかやま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kō Shrine (Q11618837)}}

panoramio (24).jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181963 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|WD=Q135159299}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11071125|lt=Q11071125|WD=Q11071125}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9200005009982 == Commons category (P373) == * Kōjinja (Takayama) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Enano Shrine|lt=Enano Shrine|WD=Q135039909}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|lt=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904}} == image of interior (P5775) == * Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (14).jpg == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県高山市江名子町4946', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kōjinja (Takayama)}} [[JA:荒神社 (高山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11618837}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kō Shrine | 画像=[[File:Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (14).jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Takayama|Q622594}} Enako-chō 4946 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Hi-musubi-no-kami {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami}} etc. | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (candidate shrine) | 例祭=around January 7 of {{jalink|leap years|Q19828}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kō Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (24).jpg|thumb|250px|Kō Shrine]] '''Kō Shrine''' (こうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Enako-chō, {{jalink|Takayama|Q622594}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is also called “Aragami-sha” or “Ara-jinja.” It is a candidate shrine for Ena Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Ōno District|Q31671}}, {{jalink|Hida Province|Q1056535}}. The Shikinaisha Ena Shrine is usually taken as {{jalink|Ena Shrine|Q11618316}} in the same Enako-chō, Takayama, but this identification was made in {{jalink|Bunka era|Q1373146|Bunka}} 12 ({{jalink|1815|Q6948}}) by the Takayama kokugaku scholar {{jalink|Tanaka Ōhidesu|Q6536652}}, who identified Koyasu Daimyōjin in Inaki no Mori, Enako-chō, as the Shikinaisha Ena Shrine because “Ena” was interpreted as {{jalink|placenta|Q1212935|胞衣}}. Even now, the theory that Kō Shrine is Ena remains strong. It is located upstream on the Enako River, a tributary of the {{jalink|Miya River|Q1156518|Miya River}} in the Jinzu system. There is a theory that the place name Enako changed from Ena. The founding period is unknown, but it is said to have once been a sacred place where women were prohibited. === Enshrined deities === *Hi-musubi-no-kami *{{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami}} *Okutsu-hiko-no-Kami *Okutsu-hime-no-Kami === Annual festival === *A festival held once every four years (around Saturday/Sunday near January 7 in leap years). Also called the amazake festival. *Originally on lunar leap-year November 18, but after Meiji and the solar calendar it changed to leap-year January 7. The current schedule dates from after WWII. *{{jalink|Amazake|Q455990}} and gogoku mochi (rice, barley, soybeans, chestnuts, azuki beans) are served to worshippers. *Formerly, a {{jalink|shimenawa|Q1887421}} was hung in a rice field, rice was cooked over a fire, amazake was prepared there and left overnight in the field to keep warm, and the quality next morning was used to divine that year’s harvest. Today amazake is prepared in front of Kami-Enako Community Hall. === Cultural properties === *Meoto cedar trees of Kō Shrine (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/data/kojinjyameotosugi.htm |title=荒神社の夫婦スギ |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-21}} === Access === *About 5 km southeast from {{jalink|Takayama Station|Q874020}} on the {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}}. **About 1 km north of Hida Furusato Tunnel. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|Q11665887}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや たかやま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kō Shrine (Q11618837)}}

Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Takayama|Q622594}} Enako-chō 4946 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Hi-musubi-no-kami {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami}} etc. | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (candidate shrine) | 例祭=around January 7 of {{jalink|leap years|Q19828}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kō Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (24).jpg|thumb|250px|Kō Shrine]] '''Kō Shrine''' (こうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Enako-chō, {{jalink|Takayama|Q622594}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is also called “Aragami-sha” or “Ara-jinja.” It is a candidate shrine for Ena Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Ōno District|Q31671}}, {{jalink|Hida Province|Q1056535}}. The Shikinaisha Ena Shrine is usually taken as {{jalink|Ena Shrine|Q11618316}} in the same Enako-chō, Takayama, but this identification was made in {{jalink|Bunka era|Q1373146|Bunka}} 12 ({{jalink|1815|Q6948}}) by the Takayama kokugaku scholar {{jalink|Tanaka Ōhidesu|Q6536652}}, who identified Koyasu Daimyōjin in Inaki no Mori, Enako-chō, as the Shikinaisha Ena Shrine because “Ena” was interpreted as {{jalink|placenta|Q1212935|胞衣}}. Even now, the theory that Kō Shrine is Ena remains strong. It is located upstream on the Enako River, a tributary of the {{jalink|Miya River|Q1156518|Miya River}} in the Jinzu system. There is a theory that the place name Enako changed from Ena. The founding period is unknown, but it is said to have once been a sacred place where women were prohibited. === Enshrined deities === *Hi-musubi-no-kami *{{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami}} *Okutsu-hiko-no-Kami *Okutsu-hime-no-Kami === Annual festival === *A festival held once every four years (around Saturday/Sunday near January 7 in leap years). Also called the amazake festival. *Originally on lunar leap-year November 18, but after Meiji and the solar calendar it changed to leap-year January 7. The current schedule dates from after WWII. *{{jalink|Amazake|Q455990}} and gogoku mochi (rice, barley, soybeans, chestnuts, azuki beans) are served to worshippers. *Formerly, a {{jalink|shimenawa|Q1887421}} was hung in a rice field, rice was cooked over a fire, amazake was prepared there and left overnight in the field to keep warm, and the quality next morning was used to divine that year’s harvest. Today amazake is prepared in front of Kami-Enako Community Hall. === Cultural properties === *Meoto cedar trees of Kō Shrine (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/data/kojinjyameotosugi.htm |title=荒神社の夫婦スギ |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-21}} === Access === *About 5 km southeast from {{jalink|Takayama Station|Q874020}} on the {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}}. **About 1 km north of Hida Furusato Tunnel. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|Q11665887}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや たかやま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kō Shrine (Q11618837)}}

panoramio (14).jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}{{jalink|Takayama|Q622594}} Enako-chō 4946 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Hi-musubi-no-kami {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami}} etc. | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (candidate shrine) | 例祭=around January 7 of {{jalink|leap years|Q19828}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kō Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (24).jpg|thumb|250px|Kō Shrine]] '''Kō Shrine''' (こうじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Enako-chō, {{jalink|Takayama|Q622594}}, {{jalink|Gifu Prefecture|Q131277}}. It is also called “Aragami-sha” or “Ara-jinja.” It is a candidate shrine for Ena Shrine, a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} of {{jalink|Ōno District|Q31671}}, {{jalink|Hida Province|Q1056535}}. The Shikinaisha Ena Shrine is usually taken as {{jalink|Ena Shrine|Q11618316}} in the same Enako-chō, Takayama, but this identification was made in {{jalink|Bunka era|Q1373146|Bunka}} 12 ({{jalink|1815|Q6948}}) by the Takayama kokugaku scholar {{jalink|Tanaka Ōhidesu|Q6536652}}, who identified Koyasu Daimyōjin in Inaki no Mori, Enako-chō, as the Shikinaisha Ena Shrine because “Ena” was interpreted as {{jalink|placenta|Q1212935|胞衣}}. Even now, the theory that Kō Shrine is Ena remains strong. It is located upstream on the Enako River, a tributary of the {{jalink|Miya River|Q1156518|Miya River}} in the Jinzu system. There is a theory that the place name Enako changed from Ena. The founding period is unknown, but it is said to have once been a sacred place where women were prohibited. === Enshrined deities === *Hi-musubi-no-kami *{{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami}} *Okutsu-hiko-no-Kami *Okutsu-hime-no-Kami === Annual festival === *A festival held once every four years (around Saturday/Sunday near January 7 in leap years). Also called the amazake festival. *Originally on lunar leap-year November 18, but after Meiji and the solar calendar it changed to leap-year January 7. The current schedule dates from after WWII. *{{jalink|Amazake|Q455990}} and gogoku mochi (rice, barley, soybeans, chestnuts, azuki beans) are served to worshippers. *Formerly, a {{jalink|shimenawa|Q1887421}} was hung in a rice field, rice was cooked over a fire, amazake was prepared there and left overnight in the field to keep warm, and the quality next morning was used to divine that year’s harvest. Today amazake is prepared in front of Kami-Enako Community Hall. === Cultural properties === *Meoto cedar trees of Kō Shrine (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/data/kojinjyameotosugi.htm |title=荒神社の夫婦スギ |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-21}} === Access === *About 5 km southeast from {{jalink|Takayama Station|Q874020}} on the {{jalink|Takayama Main Line|Q1188357}}. **About 1 km north of Hida Furusato Tunnel. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|Q11665887}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや たかやま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kō Shrine (Q11618837)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

panoramio (14).jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Takayama|Q622594|WD=Q622594}} Enako-chō 4946 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Hi-musubi-no-kami {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami}} etc. | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (candidate shrine) | 例祭=around January 7 of {{ill|lt=leap years|Q19828|WD=Q19828}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kō Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (24).jpg|thumb|250px|Kō Shrine]] '''Kō Shrine''' (こうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Enako-chō, {{ill|lt=Takayama|Q622594|WD=Q622594}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}. It is also called “Aragami-sha” or “Ara-jinja.” It is a candidate shrine for Ena Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671}}, {{ill|lt=Hida Province|Q1056535|WD=Q1056535}}. The Shikinaisha Ena Shrine is usually taken as {{ill|lt=Ena Shrine|Q11618316|WD=Q11618316}} in the same Enako-chō, Takayama, but this identification was made in {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1815|Q6948|WD=Q6948}}) by the Takayama kokugaku scholar {{ill|lt=Tanaka Ōhidesu|Q6536652|WD=Q6536652}}, who identified Koyasu Daimyōjin in Inaki no Mori, Enako-chō, as the Shikinaisha Ena Shrine because “Ena” was interpreted as {{ill|lt=placenta|Q1212935|WD=Q1212935|lt=胞衣}}. Even now, the theory that Kō Shrine is Ena remains strong. It is located upstream on the Enako River, a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Miya River|Q1156518|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miya River}} in the Jinzu system. There is a theory that the place name Enako changed from Ena. The founding period is unknown, but it is said to have once been a sacred place where women were prohibited. === Enshrined deities === *Hi-musubi-no-kami *{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami}} *Okutsu-hiko-no-Kami *Okutsu-hime-no-Kami === Annual festival === *A festival held once every four years (around Saturday/Sunday near January 7 in leap years). Also called the amazake festival. *Originally on lunar leap-year November 18, but after Meiji and the solar calendar it changed to leap-year January 7. The current schedule dates from after WWII. *{{ill|lt=Amazake|Q455990|WD=Q455990}} and gogoku mochi (rice, barley, soybeans, chestnuts, azuki beans) are served to worshippers. *Formerly, a {{ill|lt=shimenawa|Q1887421|WD=Q1887421}} was hung in a rice field, rice was cooked over a fire, amazake was prepared there and left overnight in the field to keep warm, and the quality next morning was used to divine that year’s harvest. Today amazake is prepared in front of Kami-Enako Community Hall. === Cultural properties === *Meoto cedar trees of Kō Shrine (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/data/kojinjyameotosugi.htm |title=荒神社の夫婦スギ |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-21}} === Access === *About 5 km southeast from {{ill|lt=Takayama Station|Q874020|WD=Q874020}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}}. **About 1 km north of Hida Furusato Tunnel. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|Q11665887|WD=Q11665887}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや たかやま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kō Shrine (Q11618837)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Takayama|Q622594|WD=Q622594}} Enako-chō 4946 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Hi-musubi-no-kami {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami}} etc. | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine) | 例祭=around January 7 of {{ill|lt=leap years|Q19828|WD=Q19828}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kō Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (24).jpg|thumb|250px|Kō Shrine]] '''Kō Shrine''' (こうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Enako-chō, {{ill|lt=Takayama|Q622594|WD=Q622594}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}. It is also called “Aragami-sha” or “Ara-jinja.” It is a candidate shrine for Ena Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671}}, {{ill|lt=Hida Province|Q1056535|WD=Q1056535}}. The Shikinaisha Ena Shrine is usually taken as {{ill|lt=Ena Shrine|Q11618316|WD=Q11618316}} in the same Enako-chō, Takayama, but this identification was made in {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1815|Q6948|WD=Q6948}}) by the Takayama kokugaku scholar {{ill|lt=Tanaka Ōhidesu|Q6536652|WD=Q6536652}}, who identified Koyasu Daimyōjin in Inaki no Mori, Enako-chō, as the Shikinaisha Ena Shrine because “Ena” was interpreted as {{ill|lt=placenta|Q1212935|WD=Q1212935|lt=胞衣}}. Even now, the theory that Kō Shrine is Ena remains strong. It is located upstream on the Enako River, a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Miya River|Q1156518|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miya River}} in the Jinzu system. There is a theory that the place name Enako changed from Ena. The founding period is unknown, but it is said to have once been a sacred place where women were prohibited. === Enshrined deities === *Hi-musubi-no-kami *{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami}} *Okutsu-hiko-no-Kami *Okutsu-hime-no-Kami === Annual festival === *A festival held once every four years (around Saturday/Sunday near January 7 in leap years). Also called the amazake festival. *Originally on lunar leap-year November 18, but after Meiji and the solar calendar it changed to leap-year January 7. The current schedule dates from after WWII. *{{ill|lt=Amazake|Q455990|WD=Q455990}} and gogoku mochi (rice, barley, soybeans, chestnuts, azuki beans) are served to worshippers. *Formerly, a {{ill|lt=shimenawa|Q1887421|WD=Q1887421}} was hung in a rice field, rice was cooked over a fire, amazake was prepared there and left overnight in the field to keep warm, and the quality next morning was used to divine that year’s harvest. Today amazake is prepared in front of Kami-Enako Community Hall. === Cultural properties === *Meoto cedar trees of Kō Shrine (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/data/kojinjyameotosugi.htm |title=荒神社の夫婦スギ |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-21}} === Access === *About 5 km southeast from {{ill|lt=Takayama Station|Q874020|WD=Q874020}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357}}. **About 1 km north of Hida Furusato Tunnel. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|Q11665887|WD=Q11665887}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや たかやま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kō Shrine (Q11618837)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Today amazake is prepared in front of Kami-Enako Community Hall. === Cultural properties === *Meoto cedar trees of Kō Shrine (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/data/kojinjyameotosugi.htm |title=荒神社の夫婦スギ |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-21}} === Access === *About 5 km southeast from {{ill|lt=Takayama Station|Q874020|WD=Q874020|1=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}. **About 1 km north of Hida Furusato Tunnel. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|Q11665887|WD=Q11665887|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや たかやま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kō Shrine (Q11618837)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

category|Category:Kōjinja (Takayama)}} [[JA:荒神社 (高山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11618837}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kō Shrine | 画像=[[File:Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (14).jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takayama|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}} Enako-chō 4946 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Hi-musubi-no-kami {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} etc. | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine) | 例祭=around January 7 of {{ill|lt=leap years|Q19828|WD=Q19828|1=Leap year}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kō Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (24).jpg|thumb|250px|Kō Shrine]] '''Kō Shrine''' (こうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Enako-chō, {{ill|lt=Takayama|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is also called “Aragami-sha” or “Ara-jinja.” It is a candidate shrine for Ena Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671|1=Ōno District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hida Province|Q1056535|WD=Q1056535|1=Hida Province}}. The Shikinaisha Ena Shrine is usually taken as {{ill|lt=Ena Shrine|Q11618316|WD=Q11618316|1=Ena Shrine}} in the same Enako-chō, Takayama, but this identification was made in {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1815|Q6948|WD=Q6948|1=1815}}) by the Takayama kokugaku scholar {{ill|lt=Tanaka Ōhidesu|Q6536652|WD=Q6536652|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}, who identified Koyasu Daimyōjin in Inaki no Mori, Enako-chō, as the Shikinaisha Ena Shrine because “Ena” was interpreted as {{ill|lt=placenta|Q1212935|WD=Q1212935|lt=胞衣|1=Placenta}}. Even now, the theory that Kō Shrine is Ena remains strong. It is located upstream on the Enako River, a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Miya River|Q1156518|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miya River|1=Jinzū River}} in the Jinzu system. There is a theory that the place name Enako changed from Ena. The founding period is unknown, but it is said to have once been a sacred place where women were prohibited. === Enshrined deities === *Hi-musubi-no-kami *{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} *Okutsu-hiko-no-Kami *Okutsu-hime-no-Kami === Annual festival === *A festival held once every four years (around Saturday/Sunday near January 7 in leap years). Also called the amazake festival. *Originally on lunar leap-year November 18, but after Meiji and the solar calendar it changed to leap-year January 7. The current schedule dates from after WWII. *{{ill|lt=Amazake|Q455990|WD=Q455990|1=Amazake}} and gogoku mochi (rice, barley, soybeans, chestnuts, azuki beans) are served to worshippers. *Formerly, a {{ill|lt=shimenawa|Q1887421|WD=Q1887421|1=Shimenawa}} was hung in a rice field, rice was cooked over a fire, amazake was prepared there and left overnight in the field to keep warm, and the quality next morning was used to divine that year’s harvest. Today amazake is prepared in front of Kami-Enako Community Hall. === Cultural properties === *Meoto cedar trees of Kō Shrine (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/data/kojinjyameotosugi.htm |title=荒神社の夫婦スギ |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-21}} === Access === *About 5 km southeast from {{ill|lt=Takayama Station|Q874020|WD=Q874020|1=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}. **About 1 km north of Hida Furusato Tunnel. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|Q11665887|WD=Q11665887|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや たかやま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kō Shrine (Q11618837)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

of interior (P5775) == * Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (14).jpg == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県高山市江名子町4946', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Kōjinja (Takayama)}} [[JA:荒神社 (高山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11618837}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kō Shrine | 画像=[[File:Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (14).jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takayama|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}} Enako-chō 4946 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Hi-musubi-no-kami {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} etc. | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine) | 例祭=around January 7 of {{ill|lt=leap years|Q19828|WD=Q19828|1=Leap year}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kō Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (24).jpg|thumb|250px|Kō Shrine]] '''Kō Shrine''' (こうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Enako-chō, {{ill|lt=Takayama|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is also called “Aragami-sha” or “Ara-jinja.” It is a candidate shrine for Ena Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671|1=Ōno District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hida Province|Q1056535|WD=Q1056535|1=Hida Province}}. The Shikinaisha Ena Shrine is usually taken as {{ill|lt=Ena Shrine|Q11618316|WD=Q11618316|1=Ena Shrine}} in the same Enako-chō, Takayama, but this identification was made in {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1815|Q6948|WD=Q6948|1=1815}}) by the Takayama kokugaku scholar {{ill|lt=Tanaka Ōhidesu|Q6536652|WD=Q6536652|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}, who identified Koyasu Daimyōjin in Inaki no Mori, Enako-chō, as the Shikinaisha Ena Shrine because “Ena” was interpreted as {{ill|lt=placenta|Q1212935|WD=Q1212935|lt=胞衣|1=Placenta}}. Even now, the theory that Kō Shrine is Ena remains strong. It is located upstream on the Enako River, a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Miya River|Q1156518|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miya River|1=Jinzū River}} in the Jinzu system. There is a theory that the place name Enako changed from Ena. The founding period is unknown, but it is said to have once been a sacred place where women were prohibited. === Enshrined deities === *Hi-musubi-no-kami *{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} *Okutsu-hiko-no-Kami *Okutsu-hime-no-Kami === Annual festival === *A festival held once every four years (around Saturday/Sunday near January 7 in leap years). Also called the amazake festival. *Originally on lunar leap-year November 18, but after Meiji and the solar calendar it changed to leap-year January 7. The current schedule dates from after WWII. *{{ill|lt=Amazake|Q455990|WD=Q455990|1=Amazake}} and gogoku mochi (rice, barley, soybeans, chestnuts, azuki beans) are served to worshippers. *Formerly, a {{ill|lt=shimenawa|Q1887421|WD=Q1887421|1=Shimenawa}} was hung in a rice field, rice was cooked over a fire, amazake was prepared there and left overnight in the field to keep warm, and the quality next morning was used to divine that year’s harvest. Today amazake is prepared in front of Kami-Enako Community Hall. === Cultural properties === *Meoto cedar trees of Kō Shrine (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/data/kojinjyameotosugi.htm |title=荒神社の夫婦スギ |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-21}} === Access === *About 5 km southeast from {{ill|lt=Takayama Station|Q874020|WD=Q874020|1=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}. **About 1 km north of Hida Furusato Tunnel. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|Q11665887|WD=Q11665887|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうしんしや たかやま}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKō Shrine (Q11618837)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kō Shrine (Q11618837)}}[[Category:qq]]

category|Category:Kōjinja (Takayama)}} [[JA:荒神社 (高山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11618837}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kō Shrine | 画像=[[File:Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (14).jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takayama|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}} Enako-chō 4946 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Hi-musubi-no-kami {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} etc. | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine) | 例祭=around January 7 of {{ill|lt=leap years|Q19828|WD=Q19828|1=Leap year}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kō Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (24).jpg|thumb|250px|Kō Shrine]] '''Kō Shrine''' (こうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Enako-chō, {{ill|lt=Takayama|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is also called “Aragami-sha” or “Ara-jinja.” It is a candidate shrine for Ena Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671|1=Ōno District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hida Province|Q1056535|WD=Q1056535|1=Hida Province}}. The Shikinaisha Ena Shrine is usually taken as {{ill|lt=Ena Shrine|Q11618316|WD=Q11618316|1=Ena Shrine}} in the same Enako-chō, Takayama, but this identification was made in {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1815|Q6948|WD=Q6948|1=1815}}) by the Takayama kokugaku scholar {{ill|lt=Tanaka Ōhidesu|Q6536652|WD=Q6536652|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}, who identified Koyasu Daimyōjin in Inaki no Mori, Enako-chō, as the Shikinaisha Ena Shrine because “Ena” was interpreted as {{ill|lt=placenta|Q1212935|WD=Q1212935|lt=胞衣|1=Placenta}}. Even now, the theory that Kō Shrine is Ena remains strong. It is located upstream on the Enako River, a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Miya River|Q1156518|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miya River|1=Jinzū River}} in the Jinzu system. There is a theory that the place name Enako changed from Ena. The founding period is unknown, but it is said to have once been a sacred place where women were prohibited. === Enshrined deities === *Hi-musubi-no-kami *{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} *Okutsu-hiko-no-Kami *Okutsu-hime-no-Kami === Annual festival === *A festival held once every four years (around Saturday/Sunday near January 7 in leap years). Also called the amazake festival. *Originally on lunar leap-year November 18, but after Meiji and the solar calendar it changed to leap-year January 7. The current schedule dates from after WWII. *{{ill|lt=Amazake|Q455990|WD=Q455990|1=Amazake}} and gogoku mochi (rice, barley, soybeans, chestnuts, azuki beans) are served to worshippers. *Formerly, a {{ill|lt=shimenawa|Q1887421|WD=Q1887421|1=Shimenawa}} was hung in a rice field, rice was cooked over a fire, amazake was prepared there and left overnight in the field to keep warm, and the quality next morning was used to divine that year’s harvest. Today amazake is prepared in front of Kami-Enako Community Hall. === Cultural properties === *Meoto cedar trees of Kō Shrine (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/data/kojinjyameotosugi.htm |title=荒神社の夫婦スギ |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-21}} === Access === *About 5 km southeast from {{ill|lt=Takayama Station|Q874020|WD=Q874020|1=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}. **About 1 km north of Hida Furusato Tunnel. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|Q11665887|WD=Q11665887|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:高山市の神社]] [[Category:飛騨国の式内社]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:qq]]

category|Category:Kōjinja (Takayama)}} [[JA:荒神社 (高山市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11618837}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kō Shrine | 画像=[[File:Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (14).jpg|250px]]
Haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Takayama|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}} Enako-chō 4946 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Hi-musubi-no-kami {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} etc. | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (candidate shrine) | 例祭=around January 7 of {{ill|lt=leap years|Q19828|WD=Q19828|1=Leap year}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kō Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Enakomachi, Takayama, Gifu Prefecture 506-0818, Japan - panoramio (24).jpg|thumb|250px|Kō Shrine]] '''Kō Shrine''' (こうじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Enako-chō, {{ill|lt=Takayama|Q622594|WD=Q622594|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is also called “Aragami-sha” or “Ara-jinja.” It is a candidate shrine for Ena Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671|1=Ōno District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hida Province|Q1056535|WD=Q1056535|1=Hida Province}}. The Shikinaisha Ena Shrine is usually taken as {{ill|lt=Ena Shrine|Q11618316|WD=Q11618316|1=Ena Shrine}} in the same Enako-chō, Takayama, but this identification was made in {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1815|Q6948|WD=Q6948|1=1815}}) by the Takayama kokugaku scholar {{ill|lt=Tanaka Ōhidesu|Q6536652|WD=Q6536652|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}, who identified Koyasu Daimyōjin in Inaki no Mori, Enako-chō, as the Shikinaisha Ena Shrine because “Ena” was interpreted as {{ill|lt=placenta|Q1212935|WD=Q1212935|lt=胞衣|1=Placenta}}. Even now, the theory that Kō Shrine is Ena remains strong. It is located upstream on the Enako River, a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Miya River|Q1156518|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miya River|1=Jinzū River}} in the Jinzu system. There is a theory that the place name Enako changed from Ena. The founding period is unknown, but it is said to have once been a sacred place where women were prohibited. === Enshrined deities === *Hi-musubi-no-kami *{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} *Okutsu-hiko-no-Kami *Okutsu-hime-no-Kami === Annual festival === *A festival held once every four years (around Saturday/Sunday near January 7 in leap years). Also called the amazake festival. *Originally on lunar leap-year November 18, but after Meiji and the solar calendar it changed to leap-year January 7. The current schedule dates from after WWII. *{{ill|lt=Amazake|Q455990|WD=Q455990|1=Amazake}} and gogoku mochi (rice, barley, soybeans, chestnuts, azuki beans) are served to worshippers. *Formerly, a {{ill|lt=shimenawa|Q1887421|WD=Q1887421|1=Shimenawa}} was hung in a rice field, rice was cooked over a fire, amazake was prepared there and left overnight in the field to keep warm, and the quality next morning was used to divine that year’s harvest. Today amazake is prepared in front of Kami-Enako Community Hall. === Cultural properties === *Meoto cedar trees of Kō Shrine (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/data/kojinjyameotosugi.htm |title=荒神社の夫婦スギ |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-21}} === Access === *About 5 km southeast from {{ill|lt=Takayama Station|Q874020|WD=Q874020|1=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}. **About 1 km north of Hida Furusato Tunnel. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|Q11665887|WD=Q11665887|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:qq]]

It is also called “Aragami-sha” or “Ara-jinja.” It is a candidate shrine for Ena Shrine, a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} of {{ill|lt=Ōno District|Q31671|WD=Q31671|1=Ōno District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Hida Province|Q1056535|WD=Q1056535|1=Hida Province}}. The Shikinaisha Ena Shrine is usually taken as {{ill|lt=Ena Shrine|Q11618316|WD=Q11618316|1=Ena Shrine}} in the same Enako-chō, Takayama, but this identification was made in {{ill|lt=Bunka era|Q1373146|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1815|Q6948|WD=Q6948|1=1815}}) by the Takayama kokugaku scholar {{ill|lt=Tanaka Ōhidesu|Q6536652|WD=Q6536652|1=Tanaka Ōhide}}, who identified Koyasu Daimyōjin in Inaki no Mori, Enako-chō, as the Shikinaisha Ena Shrine because “Ena” was interpreted as {{ill|lt=placenta|Q1212935|WD=Q1212935|lt=胞衣|1=Placenta}}. Even now, the theory that Kō Shrine is Ena remains strong. It is located upstream on the Enako River, a tributary of the {{ill|lt=Miya River|Q1156518|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miya River|1=Jinzū River}} in the Jinzu system. There is a theory that the place name Enako changed from Ena. The founding period is unknown, but it is said to have once been a sacred place where women were prohibited. === Enshrined deities === *Hi-musubi-no-kami *{{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Hi-no-Yagihayao-no-Kami|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} *Okutsu-hiko-no-Kami *Okutsu-hime-no-Kami === Annual festival === *A festival held once every four years (around Saturday/Sunday near January 7 in leap years). Also called the amazake festival. *Originally on lunar leap-year November 18, but after Meiji and the solar calendar it changed to leap-year January 7. The current schedule dates from after WWII. *{{ill|lt=Amazake|Q455990|WD=Q455990|1=Amazake}} and gogoku mochi (rice, barley, soybeans, chestnuts, azuki beans) are served to worshippers. *Formerly, a {{ill|lt=shimenawa|Q1887421|WD=Q1887421|1=Shimenawa}} was hung in a rice field, rice was cooked over a fire, amazake was prepared there and left overnight in the field to keep warm, and the quality next morning was used to divine that year’s harvest. Today amazake is prepared in front of Kami-Enako Community Hall. === Cultural properties === *Meoto cedar trees of Kō Shrine (city-designated natural monument){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/tennnennkinennbutu/data/kojinjyameotosugi.htm |title=荒神社の夫婦スギ |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-21}} === Access === *About 5 km southeast from {{ill|lt=Takayama Station|Q874020|WD=Q874020|1=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Takayama Main Line|Q1188357|WD=Q1188357|1=Takayama Main Line}}. **About 1 km north of Hida Furusato Tunnel. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|Q11665887|WD=Q11665887|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kō Shrine (Q11618837)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hida Province]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]]
